Fees.Borrow Interest: All interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).

Revenue.Borrow Interest: A portion of interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).
Revenue.Borrow Interest GHO: All 100% interest paid by GHO borrowers.
Revenue.Liquidation Fees: A portion of fees from liquidation penalty and bonuses.
Revenue.Flashloan Fees: A portion of fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors.

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Amount of interest distributed to lenders from all markets (excluding GHO).

SupplySideRevenue.Flashloan Fees: Flashloan fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are distributed to lenders.

ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest: Amount of interest distributed to lenders from all markets (excluding GHO) are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest GHO: All interest paid on GHO market are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Liquidation Fees: A portion of fees from liquidation penalty and bonuses are colected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Flashloan Fees: A portion of fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are collected by Aave treasury.

methodology.Fees: total fees paid by users from Colony DEX activities.
methodology.Revenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Revenue sources directly retained by the protocol include 50% from CAI fees (minting 0.20%, redemption 0.5%, and management 1%), 2% from early-stage platform activities (ceTokens distribution), 2% from USDC fundraised by a project, 16.66% from Colony DEX swap fees, and 30% from Validator Program activities. These funds support the protocol's ongoing development and operations.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 83.33% of the fees collected from Colony DEX transactions are distributed to liquidity providers. Additionally, 10% of ceTokens are distributed to staked liquidity providers on Colony's DEX, incentivizing them to continue providing liquidity and supporting the exchange. Additional revenue can be generated through farm rewards based on specific marketing campaigns and incentive programs.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.

Fees.bidnames: Action in the eosio.system contract to place a bid on premium (short <12 character) account names via auction.
Fees.ramfee: System account (eosio.ramfee) that collects the 0.5% fee from RAM buy/sell transactions to fund network operations.
Fees.cpuloan: REX-related action (e.g., fundcpuloan or defcpuloan) to manage funding or deferring CPU resource loans from staked tokens for temporary bandwidth boosts.
Fees.netloan: Database table (netloan) in the system contract tracking active REX loans specifically for NET bandwidth resources rented via actions like rentnet.
Fees.powerup: System action to pay a fee (in core tokens) for renting a fractional share of CPU and/or NET resources from the chain-owned pool for a configurable period (typically 24 hours), replacing staking/REX as the primary resource allocation model.

Fees.Borrow Interest: All interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).

Fees.Chainlink SVR: MEV recapture from Aave V3 Ethereum liquidations via Chainlink Smart Value Recapture infrastructure (Flashbots MEV-Share). 100% of recaptured value is forwarded to the Aave Collector. Available from April 2025.

Revenue.Borrow Interest: A portion of interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).
Revenue.Borrow Interest GHO: All 100% interest paid by GHO borrowers.

SupplySideRevenue.Flashloan Fees: Flashloan fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are distributed to lenders.

ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest: Amount of interest distributed to lenders from all markets (excluding GHO) are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest GHO: All interest paid on GHO market are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Liquidation Fees: A portion of fees from liquidation penalty and bonuses are colected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Flashloan Fees: A portion of fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Paraswap Partner Fees: Swap fees share from Paraswap from users by using Aave frontend.
ProtocolRevenue.Chainlink SVR: 100% of MEV recapture from Aave V3 Ethereum liquidations accrues to the Aave Collector.

HoldersRevenue.Token Buy Back: Aave starts buy back AAVE tokens using Aave Treasury after 9th April 2025. They bought daily basic, but there are days they didn't.

methodology.Fees: Total swap fees paid by traders. Per-pool fee rate read from PoolFactory.getFee (default sAMM 0.05%, vAMM 0.30%; customizable per pool) applied to each swap's input amount.
methodology.Revenue: veAERO holders' share of swap fees, equal to HoldersRevenue (Aerodrome's zero-leak model routes all protocol revenue to voters).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees earned by LPs staked in the gauge — forwarded to FeeVotingReward for distribution to veAERO voters. Computed per pool as fees × pool.balanceOf(gauge) / pool.totalSupply.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Unstaked LPs' pro-rata share of swap fees, claimable directly from the pool. Computed per pool as fees × (1 − stakedShare). Aerodrome v2 has no unstaked-LP rake module, so unstaked LPs keep 100% of their share.
methodology.BribesRevenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to the v2 GaugeFactory). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

Fees.Swap fees: All swap fees paid by traders on Aerodrome v2 pools.

Revenue.Holders fees: Staked-LP share of swap fees, forwarded to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.

HoldersRevenue.Holders fees: Staked-LP share of swap fees, forwarded to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.

SupplySideRevenue.LP fees: Unstaked-LP pro-rata share of swap fees, claimable directly from the pool.

BribesRevenue.External bribes: Token deposits to a pool's BribeVotingReward contract that veAERO voters claim by voting for the pool's gauge.

Fees.Pump Fun Cashback: Cashback Coins slice — paid by traders; same 0.30% slot as the creator fee, mutually exclusive per coin (Cashback Coins from Feb 2026).

methodology.Fees: Total swap fees paid by traders. Per-pool fee rate read from CLPool.fee() (tickSpacing-based default, customizable) applied to each swap's input amount.
methodology.Revenue: veAERO holders' share of swap fees, equal to HoldersRevenue (Aerodrome's zero-leak model routes all protocol revenue to voters).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Sum of (a) staked-LP fees and (b) the unstaked-LP rake (CLFactory.getUnstakedFee, default 10% of unstaked share), both routed into the gauge's CLPool.gaugeFees() accumulator. Measured on-chain as gaugeFees(toBlock) − gaugeFees(fromBlock) plus CollectFees event amounts (which drain the accumulator each Voter.distribute call).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Unstaked LPs' net share of swap fees after the rake, accruing via the pool's feeGrowthGlobal. Computed per pool as Fees − HoldersRevenue.
methodology.BribesRevenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to slipstream GaugeFactories). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

Fees.Swap fees: All swap fees paid by traders on Aerodrome Slipstream pools.

Revenue.Staked-LP fees + unstaked-LP rake: Both flow into the gauge's gaugeFees accumulator and are distributed to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.

HoldersRevenue.Staked-LP fees + unstaked-LP rake: Both flow into the gauge's gaugeFees accumulator and are distributed to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.

SupplySideRevenue.Unstaked-LP fees: Unstaked LPs' pro-rata share of swap fees, net of the unstaked-LP rake redirected to the gauge.

BribesRevenue.External bribes: Token deposits to a pool's BribeVotingReward contract that veAERO voters claim by voting for the pool's gauge.

methodology.Fees: Total fees paid by users on every swap, determined by the pool's fee tier (e.g., 0.01%, 0.05%, 0.30%, 1.00%).
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue represents the share of swap fees diverted to the protocol. This share is set on a per-pool basis and can be updated by governance. Default share is 0%.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Calculated per-pool. feeProtocol is a uint8 containing two uint4 values: feeProtocol0 (lower 4 bits) and feeProtocol1 (upper 4 bits). If feeProtocol0 = x1 and feeProtocol1 = x2, protocol revenue = Total Fees * (1/x1 + 1/x2) / 2. If only one is set, protocol revenue = Total Fees * 1/x.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: The portion of swap fees distributed to Liquidity Providers (LPs). This is (Total Fees - Protocol Revenue) for each pool.

methodology.UserFees: Yield earned on user deposits in syzUSD / yzPP / yzPrime.
methodology.Fees: Yield realised by Yuzu Money's underlying strategies and distributed to depositors via syzUSD / yzPP / yzPrime ERC-4626 share-rate appreciation.
methodology.Revenue: Zero by policy. Per Yuzu Money's docs the protocol takes no performance fee on yield; its economic accrual is via the off-chain Reserve Fund surplus/deficit buffer, which is not modelled here.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Zero by policy (see Revenue).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Full vault yield distributed to syzUSD / yzPP / yzPrime depositors via share-rate appreciation.

Fees.syzUSD Staking Yield To Stakers: Daily growth of syzUSD's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply, in yzUSD units.
Fees.yzPP First-Loss Tranche Yield To Holders: Daily growth of yzPP's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply, in USDT0 units. yzPP earns a base yield tracking syzUSD plus a protocol-funded bonus budget from the Reserve Fund.
Fees.yzPrime Yield To Holders: Daily growth of yzPrime's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply. yzPrime is the Yuzu Prime product on Monad with continuous yield distribution.

SupplySideRevenue.syzUSD Staking Yield To Stakers: yzUSD stakers receive 100% of the underlying strategy yield via syzUSD share-rate appreciation.
SupplySideRevenue.yzPP First-Loss Tranche Yield To Holders: yzPP holders receive base yield plus a protocol-funded bonus, in exchange for accepting first-loss risk.
SupplySideRevenue.yzPrime Yield To Holders: yzPrime holders receive the full Yuzu Prime yield via share-rate appreciation.

methodology.Fees: Total fees from both Auction-based Mining and Block-based Mining. Auction: Dutch auction mechanism where price doubles after each bid then decreases to 0 over 1 hour. Block: Players deploy SOL to mine blocks and earn tokens.
methodology.Revenue: Combined protocol revenue from both mining types. AUCTION (after Dec 17, 2025): 16% total - 10% buyback/burn, 3% staking, 2% liquidity, 1% dev. BLOCK: 9% total - 8% buyback/burn, 0.5% staking, 0.5% protocol fee.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol keeps (excludes staking to holders and motherlodes). AUCTION: 13% (10% buyback/burn + 2% liquidity + 1% dev). BLOCK: 8.5% (8% buyback/burn + 0.5% protocol fee).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Returned to participants. AUCTION: 84% to sellers (previous position owners). BLOCK: 91% to players (88% direct + 3% motherlodes when explode).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Staking rewards for $MACARON token holders. AUCTION: 3%. BLOCK: 0.5%. Combined from both mining mechanisms.

methodology.Fees: Sum of AMM trading fees (Swap.netFwFee), variable-rate borrower interest (AccrueInterest.interest), fixed-rate CT interest claims plus post-expiry treasury FW accrual (Coupon Token events), and liquidation penalties paid to external liquidators.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: AMM swap fees after reserve share, variable-rate interest to lenders, fixed-rate CT interest paid to users (RedeemInterest.interestOut), and liquidation penalties paid out to external liquidators.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: Total AMM trading fees from Swap events (netFwFee).
Fees.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: total interest from AccrueInterest. Fixed-rate: CT addresses from BondToken.CT() for BTs from AMM CreateNewMarket and fixed lending loan tokens; then RedeemInterest, CollectInterestFee, and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued in FW per CT.

Revenue.Token Swap Fees: Protocol reserve share of AMM trading fees from Swap.netFwToReserve.
Revenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: protocol share via market fee. Fixed-rate: CollectInterestFee and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued.

SupplySideRevenue.Token Swap Fees: AMM swap fees distributed outside protocol reserve.
SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: borrow interest to lenders. Fixed-rate: RedeemInterest to CT holders.
SupplySideRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Liquidation penalty estimated as collateral value seized minus debt repaid, converted via market oracle price.

methodology.Fees: Sum of (a) borrower interest accrued across every Lend reserve, (b) leverage trading fees from LeverageRfqEngine open/close/collateral-swap events, and (c) RFQ liquidation fees from RfqEngine.
methodology.Revenue: Leverage trading fees and RFQ liquidation fees flow to the Flying Tulip treasury via feeCollector and liqFeeCollector. Borrower interest does not retain a protocol cut on chain.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue. Leverage and liquidation fees both end up at 0x1118e1c057211306a40A4d7006C040dbfE1370Cb (treasury).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Borrower interest only. The on chain reserves accumulator collects interest, the protocol then swaps it to FT on the open market via LendEpochSettlerOperator and distributes the FT back to lenders pro rata via PositionsManager.settleEpoch. FT has a fixed total supply so nothing is minted.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Interest paid by borrowers across every reserve, estimated as borrows * IRM.irmSampleAPR(util) * windowSeconds / year.
Fees.Open Leverage Fee: feeAmount field of LeverageRfqEngine.OpenLeverageFilled and OpenLeverageFlashFilled events, denominated in the trade sellToken.
Fees.Close Leverage Fee: feeAmount field of LeverageRfqEngine.CloseLeverageFilled and CloseLeverageFlashFilled events.
Fees.RFQ Liquidation Fee: amount field of RfqEngine.LiquidationFeeCollected events, denominated in the asset being liquidated.

Revenue.Open Leverage Fee: Open-leverage fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector (Flying Tulip treasury).
Revenue.Close Leverage Fee: Close-leverage fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector.
Revenue.Collateral Swap Fee: Collateral-swap fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector.
Revenue.RFQ Liquidation Fee: Liquidation fees retained by the protocol via liqFeeCollector (treasury).

ProtocolRevenue.Open Leverage Fee: Open-leverage fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector (Flying Tulip treasury).
ProtocolRevenue.Close Leverage Fee: Close-leverage fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector.
ProtocolRevenue.Collateral Swap Fee: Collateral-swap fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector.
ProtocolRevenue.RFQ Liquidation Fee: Liquidation fees collected by RfqEngine, sent to liqFeeCollector (treasury).

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest To Lenders: Total borrower interest accrues to the on chain reserves accumulator; the protocol then buys FT on the open market and distributes it to lenders pro rata via PositionsManager.settleEpoch.

methodology.Fees: Two streams: (1) 1% service fee pulled atomically by FrenFlow's FeeCollector contract at trade settlement, and (2) Polymarket builder-fee distributions to FrenFlow's builder profile wallet, restricted to a sanctioned sender allowlist (paid in pUSD or USDC.e on a Polymarket-defined cadence).
methodology.UserFees: Service fees only — these are transferFrom'd from the trader's wallet atomically at fill time. Builder fees are excluded because Polymarket pays them on its own settlement cadence, not at the user trade.
methodology.Revenue: Service fees plus Polymarket builder distributions. All flow to FrenFlow treasury / builder wallet. No liquidity providers.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — 100% of collected fees are retained by the protocol.

Fees.Service Fees: Per-trade 1% service fee on Polymarket trades routed through FrenFlow. Denominated in USDC.e. Tracked from the `FeeCollected` event on the FeeCollector contract `0x95e47CBC5c4D9434412AF44Ade02B33613EDb787`.
Fees.Builder Fees: Polymarket builder-fee distributions to FrenFlow's builder profile wallet `0x58715321c2c6a216d1259f368c34f987a4a26b64`. Tracked as incoming `Transfer` events of pUSD, USDC.e, or USDC (native), restricted to a sanctioned sender allowlist of known Polymarket payout EOAs to avoid counting unrelated inflows as fees.

UserFees.Service Fees: Paid directly from the trader's wallet at fill.

Revenue.Service Fees: 100% of collected service fees flow to the FrenFlow treasury at `0xb9e912e55454Ce284C38ccFED5b7fbbF327E689b`.
Revenue.Builder Fees: 100% of builder distributions are retained by FrenFlow.

ProtocolRevenue.Service Fees: Same as Revenue — fully retained by the protocol.
ProtocolRevenue.Builder Fees: Same as Revenue — fully retained by the protocol.

methodology.Revenue: ZRO protocol fees moved from UltraLightNodeV2 to the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract via WithdrawZRO. Historically the V1 protocol take rate has been ~0, so this value is typically negligible. Tokens flow to the Foundation treasury and are not burnt by the contract.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: ZRO protocol fees collected by the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Native fees paid out to V1 relayers and oracles via WithdrawNative. Includes a small protocol-native portion when treasury chooses native payment, but in practice the protocol take is ~0 so this is effectively all worker revenue.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue. Fees accumulate in the engine and the owner sweeps them to the treasury (0x1118e1c057211306a40A4d7006C040dbfE1370Cb).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Not applicable. ftUSD does not pay yield to holders directly. Stakers earn separately via sftUSD/EpochRewardsVault, funded by FT bought on the open market with these fees.

methodology.Fees: Fees extracted per round: 1% admin fee on the total ETH deployed and 10% vault fee on losers pool after admin. Variable effective rate depending on winner/loser ratio.
methodology.Revenue: Includes all extracted fees (1% admin fee + 10% vault fee).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Includes admin fees (1% of total deployed).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is forwarded to Treasury, then bridged to Ethereum and used by the buyback bot to swap ETH for SpiceETH on Uniswap — 90% burned, 10% bridged back to Arbitrum and distributed to SPICE stakers via Treasury.distributeYield → Staking.

Fees.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.
Fees.Admin Fees: 1% admin fee on the total amount of ETH deployed on each round.

Revenue.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.

methodology.Volume: Total wager amount placed across all 6 on-chain casino game contracts on HyperEVM.
methodology.Fees: Gross gaming revenue (GGR): total wagers minus total payouts. Can be negative on days when players win more than they wager.
methodology.UserFees: Same as Fees — represents the net cost to players.
methodology.Revenue: Same as Fees. All bankroll liquidity is protocol-owned, so 100% of GGR accrues to the protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Fees. The protocol is the sole bankroll liquidity provider.

methodology.Volume: Sum of output token amounts from QuoteSettled events emitted by the Deluthium settlement contract.

methodology.UserFees: Same as Fees — all swap fees are paid by users.
methodology.Fees: Sum of `fee_amount` parsed from the Anchor `Swap` event payload that the Cube program emits via `emit!()` into program logs. The event encodes both the total swap fee and the protocol's share — see the Anchor IDL at https://github.com/cubee-ee for the exact layout. Fees are accumulated per input-token mint and priced via the DefiLlama price layer.
methodology.Revenue: Sum of `protocol_fee_amount` (the protocol's share of `fee_amount`) parsed from the same Anchor `Swap` event payload.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — the protocol's share accrues to the Cube protocol fees authority PDA on-chain.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: `fee_amount - protocol_fee_amount` per swap — the LPs' share of the swap fee.
methodology.Volume: Sum of `amount_in` parsed directly from on-chain Cube `swap` instructions on Solana, attributed to the input token mint. Computed via Dune SQL over `solana.instruction_calls` filtered by program id and the swap-instruction discriminator.

methodology.Fees: Sum of two SOL streams collected at every round reset on the Jade board: (1) a 1% admin fee on round.total_deployed forwarded to the Jade fee-collector multisig FW4UFt5nDKE2DLVNj979rXjFkjCdmzs9344JetX8hY9P, and (2) the vault portion (~9% of winnings, or 100% of deployed in no-winner rounds) forwarded to the Jade Treasury PDA F4Uwd5sQT8go5r6iiejrVc2iurYSc2dA4RKvX3cLB8e3.
methodology.Revenue: All collected SOL is protocol revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 1% admin fee on each round's total_deployed, accruing to the Jade fee-collector multisig.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Vault SOL flowing to the Jade Treasury PDA, later spent on JADE buyback-and-burn (90% burned, 10% paid to JADE stakers).

methodology.Volume: Includes ETH paid by users when conquering continents in the Land Bid game.
methodology.Fees: For V2, includes 100% of Conquer.price paid by users into WorldMiner for the core gameplay action. Historical V1 data is kept under the previous 15% protocol fee methodology.
methodology.Revenue: For V2, includes protocol revenue plus holders revenue, equal to 15% of Conquer.price. Historical V1 revenue is kept under the previous adapter methodology.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: For V2, includes 11.25% used for LAND buybacks and 0.75% used for incentives. Historical V1 protocol revenue includes protocol and protocol-owned-liquidity fees.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: For V2, includes the previous holder payout, currently 85% of each Conquer.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: For V2, includes the staking distribution, currently 3% of each Conquer.

Fees.Protocol Fees: Historical V1 protocol fee: 5% of the conquer amount.
Fees.LP Fees: Fees earned by protocol-owned/locked Uniswap V3 liquidity.
Fees.Fees to protocol owned liquidity: Historical V1 protocol-owned-liquidity fee: 10% of the conquer amount.
Fees.Conquer payments: V2: 100% of Conquer.price paid by users into WorldMiner.

Revenue.LP Fees: Fees earned by protocol-owned/locked Uniswap V3 liquidity.
Revenue.Conquer revenue: V2: protocol revenue plus holders revenue, equal to 15% of Conquer.price.

ProtocolRevenue.LP Fees: Fees earned by protocol-owned/locked Uniswap V3 liquidity.
ProtocolRevenue.Buybacks and incentives: V2: 11.25% of Conquer.price used for buybacks plus 0.75% used for incentives.

SupplySideRevenue.Previous holder payout: V2: 85% of each Conquer paid to the previous continent holder.

HoldersRevenue.Staking distribution: V2: 3% of each Conquer distributed to the LAND staking contract.

methodology.Fees: 1.25% on every buy/sell trade and 3% on every borrow (gross principal). Includes both bonding-curve trading fees and lending fees.
methodology.Revenue: 75% of total fees collected by the rise team wallet (on-chain protocol share, before any internal split).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 75% of total fees collected by the rise team wallet (on-chain protocol share, before any internal split).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 25% of total fees paid to creators and the per-market floor reserve (split per-market by creator_fee_percent).

Fees.Trading Fees: 1.25% of trade notional on buy/sell/create transactions.
Fees.Borrow Fees: 3% of gross borrow principal taken at borrow time.

Revenue.Trading fees to team wallet: 75% of trade fees flowing to the rise team wallet.
Revenue.Borrow fees to team wallet: 75% of borrow fees flowing to the rise team wallet.

ProtocolRevenue.Trading fees to team wallet: 75% of trade fees ending up in the rise treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Borrow fees to team wallet: 75% of borrow fees ending up in the rise treasury.

SupplySideRevenue.Trading fees to creator and floor reserv: 25% of trade fees split between the market creator and the market's floor reserve.
SupplySideRevenue.Borrow fees to creator and floor reserve: 25% of borrow fees split between the market creator and the market's floor reserve.

methodology.Volume: Volume is the sum of tokenOut amounts from SwapExecuted events emitted by the DEXR FeeAggregatorMaster contract on each supported chain (Base, Optimism, MegaETH, BNB Chain, HyperEVM). tokenOut represents the asset received by the user after the swap completes. Note: this counts only swaps that route through the on-chain Master contract; cross-chain bridges and same-chain swaps that fall back to LI.FI's router are not included here (DEXR is a direct LI.FI integrator but those flows accrue separately).
methodology.Fees: DEXR charges a flat 0.3% protocol fee on the input token (tokenIn) of every swap. The feeAmount field of the SwapExecuted event captures this exactly. The same 0.3% rate applies to LI.FI-routed flows but those fees are collected via the LI.FI integrator portal, not on-chain — and are not counted here.
methodology.Revenue: 100% of the 0.3% protocol fee flows to a Gnosis Safe multisig (no token holders, no buyback). The same Safe address (0xD55cE54Ce3e0985867CD57f4266c27a5b060D665) is replicated across all chains. Revenue equals fees.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: 0.3% swap fee charged on input token before swap

Revenue.Token Swap Fees: 0.3% swap fee charged on input token before swap

ProtocolRevenue.Token Swap Fees: 0.3% swap fee charged on input token before swap

methodology.UserFees: 3 basis points (0.0003) × daily volume. Flat per-fill rate, no taker/maker split, no tier ladder.
methodology.Fees: 3 basis points (0.0003) × daily volume. Flat per-fill rate, no taker/maker split, no tier ladder.
methodology.Revenue: 0. The Sour protocol does not claim any portion of the fee — 100% routes to the SOUR LP vault.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 100% of fees. The LP vault is the economic owner of the protocol; LPs receive all fee flow.

Fees.Trading Fees: 3 basis points (0.0003) × daily volume. Flat per-fill rate, no taker/maker split, no tier ladder.

SupplySideRevenue.Trading fees to SOUR LP vault: 3 basis points (0.0003) × daily volume. Flat per-fill rate, no taker/maker split, no tier ladder.

methodology.Revenue: All collected fees retained by the protocol-controlled wallets are counted as protocol revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All collected fees accrue to protocol-controlled wallets (marketplace fee collector and game treasury).

Fees.In-Game Services Fees: In-game transaction fees (store purchases, etc.) collected in MegaETH and routed to the game treasury wallet.

ProtocolRevenue.Marketplace Trading Fees: Marketplace fees accruing to the protocol-controlled fee collector wallet.
ProtocolRevenue.In-Game Services Fees: In-game fees accruing to the protocol-controlled game treasury wallet.

methodology.Fees: Total cbBTC distributed by the ClawdStrategy Treasury to STRI holders. Represents the gross income generated by the AI trading agents that is paid out to the protocol's tokenholders.
methodology.Revenue: Same as Fees. ClawdStrategy does not charge a supply-side fee — 100% of agent-generated income is paid out from the Treasury, so dailyRevenue equals dailyFees.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: All cbBTC income generated by the AI trading agents is sent from the Treasury to STRI holders pro-rata. Pre-max-supply (500,000 STRI), 100% of distributions go to holders. After the supply cap is reached, the protocol begins retaining 5% for ongoing operations.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 0 today. The protocol takes 0% until the 500,000 STRI max supply is fully minted; afterwards, 5% of distributions are allocated to protocol operations.

methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Includes NFTs burned from protocol revenue and staker fees distributed to the NFT stakers
methodology.Volume: Sum of buy totalCost and sell grossPayout from NFTBought + NFTSold events across every AMM vault deployed by the Clutch Anvil factory.

Fees.Trading Fees: Sum of protocolFee + stakerFee fields from NFTBought + NFTSold events. Protocol fee is burned; staker fee streams to the NFT staking vault as rewards.

SupplySideRevenue.Fees to NFT burn: Sum of protocolFee fields from NFTBought + NFTSold events. Protocol fee is burned directly rewarding NFT holders (supply side)
SupplySideRevenue.Fees to NFT stakers: Sum of stakerFee fields from NFTBought + NFTSold events. Staker fee streams to the NFT staking vault as rewards.

methodology.Fees: USDC payments for DoubleZero Edge shred subscriptions, measured from settled transfers into the shred distribution flow.
methodology.Revenue: 10% of Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: No protocol revenue
methodology.HoldersRevenue: 10% of Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 90% of Edge revenue is distributed: 50% of the remainder to DoubleZero network contributors, 32.5% to shred-originating validators, and 17.5% to protocol client teams.

Fees.Edge Shred Subscriptions: USDC payments for DoubleZero Edge shred subscriptions, measured from settled transfers into the shred distribution flow.

Revenue.Protocol Security Burn: 10% of gross Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.

SupplySideRevenue.DoubleZero Network Contributors: 45% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 50% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to DoubleZero network contributors proportional to contribution.
SupplySideRevenue.Shred-Originating Validators: 29.25% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 32.5% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to shred-originating validators proportional to leader shreds.
SupplySideRevenue.Protocol Client Teams: 15.75% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 17.5% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to protocol client teams proportional to shred distribution.

HoldersRevenue.Protocol Security Burn: 10% of gross Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.

Fees.Traffic Purchase: Canton tokens burned to purchase synchronizer bandwidth.
Fees.Preapproval Burn: Canton tokens burned to create a standing authorization for receiving CC transfers without being online.
Fees.Preapproval Renew Burn: Canton tokens burned to renew an expiring preapproval before it lapses.
Fees.Setup Burn: Canton tokens burned to onboard an external party onto the network.
Fees.Dust Expire: Near-zero Amulets whose holding fees exceeded their value, garbage-collected by super validators and burned.
Fees.Holding Fee: Per-round fee accrued on Amulet UTXOs, charged upon consumption (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).
Fees.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

Revenue.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

HoldersRevenue.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

methodology.Fees: We fetch daily transaction fees from NearBlocks API. The data is aggregated daily and includes all transaction fees paid on the NEAR blockchain. 70% of transaction fees are burned, while 30% can optionally be allocated to smart contract developers as rewards if they specify a fee percentage for their contracts (otherwise 100% is burned). Note that validators do not earn transaction fees - their rewards come from protocol-level inflation.

methodology.UserFees: Users pay $0 for transactions. sFUEL (gas token) is obtained free from faucets and has no market value.
methodology.Fees: SKALE uses sFUEL (SKALE Fuel), a gas token distributed FREE via faucets. While transactions consume gas on-chain, sFUEL has $0 monetary value. User fees are $0.
methodology.Revenue: Network revenue comes from SKL token staking subscriptions by dApp developers, not from transaction fees. On-chain transaction fees are $0 as sFUEL is free.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue is generated through SKL staking model where dApp developers stake tokens for network access. Transaction fees contribute $0 as sFUEL is valueless.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Validators/nodes are compensated through SKL staking rewards, not transaction fees. On-chain gas fees in sFUEL are $0.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: No sFUEL is burned or distributed as revenue since it has no value. Revenue model is entirely based on SKL token staking.
